codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal fix. Added an autouse pytest fixture in conftest.py that creates a minimal valid tokenlist (3 tokens: DAI and USDC on Ethereum, DAI on Arbitrum) when the gitignored uniswap-default.json is absent. The exists() guard makes the per-test overhead negligible. All three expectedBehavior criteria are met: tests pass in clean checkouts, tests remain meaningful (validating JSON structure, token fields, static serving), and production behavior is unchanged.

issues.description: Fixture uses default function scope but could use session scope since the file only needs to be created once per test session. The exists() guard makes this academic — the filesystem check is cheap — but session scope would be slightly more idiomatic for one-time setup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The gitignored tokenlist file and its CI implications are not documented in .factory/library/. Future workers may be confused about why uniswap-default.json is gitignored and how tests handle its absence. A short note in library (e.g., in architecture.md or a new file) explaining the Droid-Shield false-positive reason for gitignoring it and the conftest.py fixture solution would save future debugging time.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .gitignore last line: 'static/tokenlists/uniswap-default.json'. Mission AGENTS.md mentions 'The Uniswap default tokenlist is served from static/tokenlists/uniswap-default.json' but not the gitignore/CI concern. The repo AGENTS.md has no mention of this either.

summary: PASS. The worker added a well-structured autouse pytest fixture that creates a minimal valid tokenlist when the gitignored file is absent, fixing CI failures in clean checkouts. The implementation is clean (single file, 50 lines added), correctly placed in conftest.py, and all verification steps confirmed passing. One non-blocking suggestion: session scope would be marginally more efficient. One knowledge gap observation: the gitignore/CI fixture pattern should be documented in .factory/library/ for future workers.
